title: Head of Revenue Operations
location: New York, NY (with Tel Aviv HQ collaboration)
description: Reporting to the CRO, this high-impact leadership role builds and scales the operational backbone of Finout's go-to-market engine as the company enters its 2026 growth phase. Requires 7+ years in Revenue Operations, Sales Ops, or Strategy within high-growth B2B SaaS, experience scaling RevOps beyond $30M+ ARR, deep Salesforce administration and revenue analytics expertise, and cross-functional leadership across Sales, Marketing, Finance, and HR. Finout is a cloud cost management company with 100+ people across Tel Aviv and New York, backed by $85M+ in funding and trusted by brands like Wiz, Elastic, and Lyft.
score_reason: Perfect alignment across all dimensions: 15+ years exceeds 7+ requirement, proven RevOps scaling experience matches $30M+ ARR threshold, Salesforce expertise confirmed, fintech/SaaS background transfers directly to cloud cost management, and cross-functional leadership capabilities match CRO-reporting scope.
ai_opener: I've spent 15 years building RevOps engines for scaling B2B SaaS companies, and Finout's position in the cloud cost management space—especially with your $85M+ funding and 100+ person team entering 2026 growth—aligns perfectly with my experience scaling revenue operations beyond $30M ARR. Your Tel Aviv-NYC structure is particularly interesting given my familiarity with Israeli tech operations, and I'm excited about the opportunity to build the operational backbone that powers GTM at this scale.

title: RevOps Manager, Territory Strategy & Operations
description: As RevOps Manager focused on Territory Strategy & Operations, you'll accelerate revenue growth and seller productivity through data-driven investment strategy, scalable territory design, and consultative partnership with business leaders. Requires 3–5+ years in Revenue Operations, Sales Strategy, or GTM planning, deep understanding of B2B SaaS go-to-market models and segmentation, strong analytical skills with Excel, SQL, and BI tools (Tableau, Retool), plus CRM proficiency, especially Salesforce. Experience with territory management tools like Fullcast, Pigment, or Anaplan is a plus.
score_reason: Exceptional match: 15+ years B2B SaaS RevOps experience far exceeds 3-5 year requirement, deep Salesforce expertise, proven pipeline forecasting and GTM strategy background aligns perfectly with territory strategy focus. Only minor gap is no explicit territory management tool (Fullcast/Pigment) experience mentioned, but core analytical and operational skills are elite.
ai_opener: Omri—your RevOps Manager role at Gong caught my attention because the territory strategy mandate directly maps to my 15 years scaling B2B SaaS revenue operations, including building data-driven segmentation models and sales forecasting frameworks. I've architected similar Salesforce-based operations at high-growth companies and would bring immediate impact on both the analytical rigor and cross-functional execution your team needs.

title: GTM Engineer (Remote)
description: As Nooks' first GTM Engineer, you'll serve as the technical co-pilot for the Sales Development organization, owning setup, optimization, and enablement within the Nooks platform, and identifying and resolving process bottlenecks across systems and strategy. Requires 2+ years of relevant sales development experience selling into sales leaders, experience prospecting into B2B SaaS mid-market/enterprise companies, strong analytic skills, and deep familiarity with tools like Nooks, HubSpot, ZoomInfo, and Sales Nav. Nooks is an AI Sales Assistant Platform backed by $70M+ from top VCs including Kleiner Perkins.
score_reason: Strong alignment on GTM/RevOps expertise, HubSpot proficiency, and B2B SaaS knowledge. However, the role appears more SDR-focused and tactical than Omri's senior executive level, and lacks explicit fintech/AI-native GTM emphasis that drives his highest engagement.
ai_opener: Omri here—15+ years building GTM engines at scale in B2B SaaS. Your first GTM Engineer role at Nooks caught my attention: the opportunity to architect sales development infrastructure for an AI-native platform resonates with how I've scaled RevOps motions. I've deep expertise in HubSpot optimization and pipeline orchestration that could accelerate your SDR organization's maturity.

title: GTM Engineer
description: Conveo seeks someone who can code, ship, and iterate on the internal tools and automations that make their GTM team effective as the company scales rapidly. You'll replace manual workflows and spreadsheets with purpose-built apps, pipelines, and AI-powered workflows—lead scoring models that learn, attribution pipelines, onboarding flows, and AI agents that eliminate hours of manual work weekly. Conveo is an AI research platform enabling fast, affordable, high-quality consumer/B2B research, used by global brands like Unilever, Google, and Orange.
score_reason: Strong alignment on GTM automation, pipeline workflows, and AI-native tooling—core RevOps strengths. However, the role emphasizes coding/shipping which isn't Omri's primary strength as an exec, and Conveo's B2B research focus (vs. SaaS product itself) is slightly tangential to his core fintech/SaaS GTM experience.
ai_opener: Omri here—I've spent 15+ years building scalable GTM engines for B2B SaaS, and I'm intrigued by Conveo's mission to automate research at scale. Your vision of replacing spreadsheets with AI-powered workflows (lead scoring, attribution, onboarding) directly mirrors the RevOps stack I've architected—especially the demand gen automation and pipeline intelligence pieces. Given you're scaling rapidly with enterprise customers like Unilever and Google, I'd love to explore how someone who's optimized GTM operations across hypergrowth could accelerate your internal tooling roadmap.

title: Director of Revenue Operations
description: Partner with executive leadership to develop forecasting and predictive models supporting growth planning, resource allocation, and revenue confidence. You'll collaborate with Sales and Marketing to improve targeting, optimize lead handoffs, measure campaign effectiveness, and align incentives with revenue goals. Requires 8–12+ years in Revenue Operations or related functions (Sales Ops, Marketing Ops, BizOps), ideally in high-growth B2B SaaS or fintech, and proven success supporting multiple sales motions including PLG. Highbeam is building the future of business banking, backed by $42M from Acrew, FirstMark, Mayfield, and Two Sigma Ventures.
score_reason: Exceptional match—15+ years in B2B SaaS RevOps exceeds the 8–12 year requirement, fintech expertise aligns perfectly with Highbeam's business banking focus, and forecasting/pipeline modeling + multi-motion sales (including PLG) are core strengths. Remote location is ideal.
ai_opener: Omri, I came across Highbeam's Series B and was impressed by the team's vision for business banking—especially given the complexity of forecasting across PLG + Sales motions at scale. With your 15+ years building RevOps functions in high-growth B2B SaaS and fintech, you've likely encountered the exact challenges Highbeam's leadership is solving for around predictive modeling and sales/marketing alignment. I think a conversation about how you'd architect their forecasting infrastructure could be valuable.
